Mid Product Designer

We're seeking a Mid Product Designer to join our Design team and help
shape the next generation of data visualization tools. You'll work cross-
functionally with product managers, engineers, and data scientists to
design intuitive interfaces for complex analytics workflows. This role is

ideal for a designer with 2-5 years of professional experience who has

shipped consumer or enterprise products and is ready to take on greater
ownership and strategic influence.

About the Company

Lattice Labs is a B2B SaaS company focused on building modern analytics
and data visualization platforms for enterprises. We believe that complex

data shouldn't require complex interfaces. Our team of designers,
engineers, and data scientists collaborate to create beautiful, accessible

products that empower business intelligence professionals worldwide.

What You'll Own

1. Design end-to-end user experiences for new analytics features, from

discovery through implementation, using Figma to create high-fidelity
prototypes and design specifications

2. Collaborate with product managers and engineers in bi-weekly design
critiques to iterate on solutions, incorporating feedback and data-
driven insights

3. Contribute to and maintain our design system in Figma, documenting

component patterns, interaction states, and accessibility guidelines

for use across the platform

4. Conduct user research sessions (interviews, usability testing) to

validate design decisions and identify opportunities for improvement
in data visualization workflows

5. Create interactive prototypes using Figma's prototyping tools and

Framer to demonstrate interaction design concepts and user flows to

at Lattice Labs — Designing intuitive experiences that connect people todataSan Francisco, CA (Remote-friendly) • Full-time • $125,000 - $155,000 USD annually stakeholders

6. Work closely with frontend engineers to ensure design specifications

are accurately implemented, troubleshooting pixel-perfect details and

interaction timing

7. Mentor junior designers on the team by providing design feedback,

suggesting Figma best practices, and sharing knowledge about design
systems principles

8. Participate in design system governance by proposing and evaluating

new components, ensuring consistency across the product suite

Required

Preferred

Experience designing for
enterprise or data-heavy
applications
Familiarity with accessibility
standards (WCAG 2.1) and
inclusive design practices
Knowledge of front-end
technologies (React, HTML/CSS)

or ability to collaborate
effectively with engineers
Experience with design tools
beyond Figma (Sketch, Adobe XD)
or prototyping tools like
Framer or Principle

2-5 years of professional
product design experience,
preferably in SaaS, analytics,
or B2B software
Proficiency in Figma, including
component libraries, variants,
prototyping, and design tokens
Strong portfolio demonstrating

experience with interaction
design and multi-step user
workflows
Experience building or
contributing to design systems
and understanding of atomic
design principles
Solid foundation in UX research
methodologies and ability to

articulate design decisions
based on user needs
Excellent communication skills
with the ability to present
design work to cross-functional
teams and stakeholders

Technologies

Figma   Interaction Design   Design Systems   Prototyping   Framer

Component-based Architecture   User Research & Testing   Accessibility (WCAG)

Design Tokens   Cross-functional Collaboration

 Perks & Benefits

Competitive salary and comprehensive health benefits (medical, dental,
vision)
401(k) retirement plan with 4% employer match
Generous PTO policy: 4 weeks paid time off, 10 company holidays, and
flexible sick days
Professional development budget: $2,000 annually for conferences,
courses, and design tools
Collaborative workspace with standing desks, design studios, and quiet

focus areas
Stock options available to all employees
Unlimited coffee, snacks, and quarterly team events
Mental health support through subsidized therapy sessions and wellness
programs

Next Steps

Submit your resume, cover letter, and a link to your portfolio (preferably

Figma case studies) to careers@latticelabs.io. Please include a brief note
(2-3 sentences) about a design system or interaction design project you're
proud of. We review applications on a rolling basis and aim to respond
within 2 weeks.
